page_content:
ENEE140 Introduction to Programming Concepts for Engineers (2 Credits)

Introduction to the programming environment: editing, compiling, UNIX, data types and variable scope; program selection, formatted/unformatted input/output, repetition, functions, arrays and strings.

Prerequisite: Permission of ENGR-Electrical & Computer Engineering department.

Restriction: Must be in Engineering: Electrical program; or must be in Engineering: Materials Science program.

ENEE150 Intermediate Programming Concepts for Engineers (3 Credits) Advanced programming concepts: coding conventions and style; pointers; dynamic memory allocation and data structures; linked lists; graphs; abstract data types; object-oriented design. There will be team- based software projects and group presentations.

Prerequisite: Permission of ENGR-Electrical & Computer Engineering department. And ENEE140 or CMSC131; or score of 5 on the A Java

AP exam; or score of 4 or 5 on the AB Java AP exam; or satisfactory performance on the department's placement exam.
